scenario: Trump 'leaving in 2-3 weeks' sparks tactical Brent sell-off; Tehran Toll Booth active - Iran attacking Israel + Kuwait same day

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Fertilizer cost pass-through: Gulf urea +90% since Hormuz closure raises planting costs across Asia rice belts. Egypt (Hormuz-exposed wheat buyer) panic-buying elevates global benchmark. Trump 'leaving' signal modestly reduces upside; Iran attacks ongoing - no supply chain resolution. Source: Barchart ZWK26, StockPil Mar 2026.

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel demand spike (SE Asia) as jet fuel prices ease slightly on Trump signal. Indonesian ethanol mandate acceleration competes with feed use. Fertilizer cost uplift on next planting cycle persists; Tehran Toll Booth still active.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker cost spike moderating on Trump signal (+38% on Asia-origin routes vs +42% prior). Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins under continued pressure from elevated freight. Limited direct Hormuz exposure - US/South America origin - caps upside vs energy commodities.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: Crude oil correlation driving CPO lower on Trump 'leaving' signal (Brent -13%). Biodiesel feedstock demand still elevated; Indonesia B40 mandate intact. Fertilizer disruption threatening next Indonesian yield cycle. Iran attacks Kuwait and Israel Apr 1 - no structural resolution. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security alarm: fertilizer shortfall threatens Vietnam and Thailand rice belts. Philippines, Indonesia importing at distressed freight premiums. India export ban in place. Trump signal does not resolve planting-cycle fertilizer shortage already locked in.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Gulf condensate-derived naphtha tracking Brent lower on Trump 'leaving' signal (Brent -13%). Tehran Toll Booth still active; Qatar force majeure unresolved; IRGC threatens 18 US tech firms Apr 1. Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut crackers remain at reduced rates. Source: SGX NJF1 futures.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Downstream from naphtha easing. SABIC and QAPCO ethylene exports still bottlenecked; partial naphtha relief passes through with a lag. SE Asian PE and PVC producers see marginal cost improvement; agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times still 6-8 weeks. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia.

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Iran is world's 2nd largest methanol exporter - production disrupted directly by conflict. Qatar force majeure in effect. China MTO complex facing feedstock squeeze. Trump signal reduces upside slightly; Iran attacks ongoing Apr 1 - no production restart. Partial offset from US and Chinese domestic supply. Source: Investing.com CFR China futures.

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Qafco (Qatar) force majeure in effect since Mar 2. Saudi SAFCO and SABIC urea plants at ~60% capacity. Trump signal moderates upside slightly but Iran fires 7 missiles at Israel Apr 1 - no production restart path. Iranian parliament still legislating Tehran Toll Booth - Hormuz access structurally uncertain. Egypt and India panic-buying from Russia/China at large premiums. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers.

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: Saudi Ma'aden DAP shipping disrupted; Toll Booth transit cost adds $20-30/mt delivered premium. US Gulf filling gap at premium freight. Philippines and Vietnam facing import cost spikes ahead of wet-season planting.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Less directly Hormuz-exposed (Canada, Russia main suppliers) but elevated freight costs add indirect pressure. Trump signal reduces war premium marginally. Palm oil plantations (Indonesia, Malaysia) are major users - any yield disruption amplifies CPO supply concern.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: Cape re-routing absorbing dry bulk vessel capacity as iron ore, coal, and grain shipments divert around southern Africa. Australia-Japan coal routing Cape adding 12 days. Trump signal does not immediately reduce routing demand - vessels already committed to Cape. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News.

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: Tehran Toll Booth still active ($2M/vessel in yuan); VLCC owners face dual pricing structure. Trump 'leaving' signal reduces war-risk premium slightly but Iran fires 7 missiles at Israel Apr 1 and drones Kuwait Airport - Hormuz risk unresolved. P&I restoration impossible until political settlement. ~35% of VLCC fleet war-risk excluded. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List.

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Asia-Europe container rates easing marginally on Trump peace signal but Suez Canal services remain suspended. Dubai port congestion severe; Kuwait Airport damage adds GCC disruption vector. SE Asian manufacturers still facing extended lead times into European and Middle East markets. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange.
